paper.title: LP 543-25: a rare low-mass runaway disk star

review.summary: This RNAAS Research Note revisits the kinematics of the high proper-motion star LP 543-25 (PSS 544-7, Gaia DR2 3137866242753594624) using Gaia DR2 astrometry. The authors compute heliocentric Galactic velocity components (U=206±21, V=−289±29, W=30±30 km/s) and corresponding Galactocentric velocities (U=−195±21, V=−34±29, W=37±30 km/s), finding the star is bound to the Galaxy and moving at ~200 km/s away from the Galactic Center in the plane — consistent with a dynamical ejection origin from an inner-Galaxy star cluster. The paper compares LP 543-25 with six low-mass runaway stars from Tetzlaff et al. (2011) using Gaia DR2 data, confirming LP 543-25 as one of the closest (~458 pc) and least massive runaway stars identified so far. The paper contains no structured data tables: all numerical results are presented inline in the text, and the HR diagram plus velocity-component plots appear in Figure 1. The commented-out deluxetable in the LaTeX source (lines 86–100) is an AAS RNAAS template example, not actual paper data. The acknowledgments mention SIMBAD, VizieR, NASA ADS, and Gaia mission institutional URLs; these are generic database/telescope acknowledgments, not structured data resources.
